step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the chosen option B (Oral Vancomycin). While topical treatments could manage surface bacteria effectively, oral vancomycin offers better systemic protection by addressing both nasopharyngeal and potentially other sites where MRSA might reside or spread. Additionally, using oral medication aligns well with standard infection control strategies aimed at reducing community-acquired MRSA cases associated with hospitalized patients. Therefore, my final stance supports Option B - Oral Vancomycin.
